author: luca_zanolini
content: Fair, the drift isn't free-form. The next subsection ('Block status') should make it precise: bid.parent_block_hash of any canonical block always equals the block_hash of the most recent FULL ancestor, so the two pointers can only differ by skipping consecutive EMPTY blocks. The two-pointer framing is meant to set up what's actually stored on-chain. We can certainly make this binary nature explicit since the beginning tho

author: potuz
content: Thing is that when thinking about the security properties and assumptions, it is a very common misconception even among client devs implementing this, that parents are kinda arbitrary, when in fact a block pin points a single ancestry chain which is very restrictive, you can't pick up a random pair of canonical (root, hash) and choose it as your parent. This misconception, while it may be very simple to you guys, I'm pretty certain that takes time to absorb cause I've seen it countless times, so it's better to avoid it right away
